name: python3-aioquic
title: python3-aioquic
summary: Python implementation of QUIC and HTTP/3
description:
Python implementation of QUIC and HTTP/3
Library for the QUIC network protocol in Python. It features
a minimal TLS 1.3 implementation, a QUIC stack and an HTTP/3 stack.
.
QUIC was standardised in `RFC 9000`_ and HTTP/3 in `RFC 9114`_.
``aioquic`` is regularly tested for interoperability against other
`QUIC implementations`_.
.
Features
--------
- QUIC stack conforming with `RFC 9000`_
- HTTP/3 stack conforming with `RFC 9114`_
- minimal TLS 1.3 implementation conforming with `RFC 8446`_
- IPv4 and IPv6 support
- connection migration and NAT rebinding
- logging TLS traffic secrets
- logging QUIC events in QLOG format
- HTTP/3 server push support
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-aioquic because Python implementation of QUIC and HTTP/3.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-aioquic as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-aioquic with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-aioquic --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
